content.text:
I'll analyze the two fixes needed. Both fixes target the same file (it's both the main file and the companion test file) and address the same issue: Button no longer renders `aria-disabled` attribute in PF6.

**Fix 1 & Fix 2** are essentially the same fix applied to the same file:
- Line 68-71: `toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'false')` → should become `.not.toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled')` (since the button is enabled, it won't have `aria-disabled` at all)
- Line 81-84: `toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true')` → should become `.toBeDisabled()` or `.toHaveAttribute('disabled')` (since the button is disabled, check the native `disabled` attribute)

Let me read the file first:

Now I have the full file. Let me apply both fixes:

**Line 68-71:** The button is enabled (success test case). In PF6, Button no longer renders `aria-disabled` at all when enabled. Change `toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'false')` → `.not.toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled')`.

**Line 81-84:** The button is disabled (unauthorized test case). In PF6, Button uses the native `disabled` attribute instead of `aria-disabled`. Change `toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true')` → `.toBeDisabled()`.
Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. The companion test file is the same file (self-referencing), so no additional files need updating.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Status | Details |
|-----|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1 (line 68)** | ✅ Applied | Changed `.toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'false')` → `.not.toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled')` — In PF6, Button no longer renders `aria-disabled` when enabled, so we assert the attribute is absent entirely. |
| **Fix 1 (line 81)** | ✅ Applied | Changed `.toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true')` → `.toBeDisabled()` — In PF6, disabled Button uses the native HTML `disabled` attribute instead of `aria-disabled`. |
| **Fix 2** | ✅ Applied | Same changes as Fix 1 — the companion test file is the same file, so both fixes were addressed together. |

No imports were added or removed (none became unused). No other test interactions (dropdowns, portals, OUIA selectors, role queries) were found in this file that would be affected by these changes.
